title: Postman の 422 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: Most factual claims are well-supported by recent and reputable sources, including official documentation and company blogs.
The article correctly identifies common causes and solutions for HTTP 422 errors, particularly in the context of Postman, GitHub API, and FastAPI.
The Editor's Note accurately summarizes the linked Stack Overflow discussions.
The information provided is generally up-to-date, with many sources published within the last year or two.
One claim regarding FastAPI's error message structure is inferred from general FastAPI behavior rather than a direct Postman interaction with FastAPI, but the information itself is accurate.

sources.title: What is HTTP 422 Error? (Unprocessable Entity) - Scrapfly Blog
sources.claim: HTTP 422 "Unprocessable Entity" occurs when the server understands the request but finds the data semantically invalid.

sources.title: HTTP 422 Error: How to Diagnose and Fix Unprocessable Entity Responses - Postman Blog
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity error means your API request was syntactically correct, but the server couldn't process it due to validation errors in your data. Common causes include missing required fields, incorrect data types, invalid formats, or constraint violations. Incorrect Content-Type headers: The body format doesn't match the declared content type.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Entity Explained - KeyCDN Support
sources.claim: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means the server understands the content type of the request entity and the syntax of the request entity is correct but was unable to process the contained instructions. This HTTP status was introduced in RFC 4918.

sources.title: Debug API requests in Postman
sources.claim: Every request sent by Postman is logged in the Postman Console, so you can view the detail of what happened when you sent a request, including all underlying request headers, variable values, and redirects. The Postman Console logs the primary request, proxy configuration, and certificates.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Entity - HTTP Status Codes Guides
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 status code means the server understands the response body but couldn't process the request because there's something wrong with the content.

sources.title: FastAPI Server Errors And Logs — Take Back Control | by Roy Pasternak | Medium
sources.claim: Pydantic Validation Error (422) — This is a client-side error and it returns a response with a status code 422. This error is caused by the data the client sends. FastAPI server logs can show detailed validation errors for 422.

sources.title: Http error: 422 - Unprocessable Entity - Productive Shop
sources.claim: The 422 (Unprocessable Entity) status code means that the server cannot understand the content of the request. This is generally caused by incorrect syntax in the request. RFC #: 4918 Section 11.2.

sources.title: Powerful Debugging with the Postman Console
sources.claim: Every network call sent with Postman is logged in the console in both its raw and pretty form, replacing all the variables that you've used in the request. In the console, you can inspect headers, certificates, requests, and responses.

sources.title: Understanding the 422 Unprocessable Entity Status Code | by AHMET TUNÇ - Medium
sources.claim: The 422 Unprocessable Entity status code indicates that the server understands the content type of the request, and the syntax of the request is correct, but the server cannot process the contained instructions. This status code is typically used in RESTful APIs, particularly when dealing with user input that fails validation.

sources.title: The Postman Console
sources.claim: Postman Console logs the actual request that was sent, including all underlying request headers, and the exact response sent by the server.

sources.title: Postman Beginner Tutorial 12 | How to Debug - YouTube
sources.claim: The Postman Console logs the following information: The primary request that was sent, including all underlying request headers, variable values, and redirects. The proxy configuration and certificates used for the request. Network information, and log statements and asynchronous requests from test or pre-request scripts.

sources.title: API Debugging - Postman Quickstarts
sources.claim: The Postman console provides visibility into network calls and logs. It is a simple, yet powerful, way to spot bugs.

sources.title: 400 Bad Request vs 422 Unprocessable Entity - Beeceptor
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity status code means that while the server was able to interpret the request sent, it is still not able to process it due to some logical error. This error is used when the request syntax is correct, but the data fails validation or business rules.

sources.title: GitHub API - 422: Unprocessable Entity: Review comments is invalid and Review threads is invalid · community · Discussion #42272 · GitHub
sources.claim: The HTTP 422 error in the GitHub REST API means "Validation failed, or the endpoint has been spammed." This typically occurs when the parameters provided in the request are invalid or do not meet the requirements of the API endpoint. The response body of the 422 error often contains details about what went wrong.

sources.title: Rename 422 from "Unprocessable Entity" to "Unprocessable Content" · Issue #664 · hyperium/http - GitHub
sources.claim: RFC 9110 (June 2022) defines 422 as "Unprocessable Content," indicating that the server understands the content type and syntax, but cannot process the contained instructions. RFC 4918 (June 2007) defines it as "Unprocessable Entity."

sources.title: Troubleshooting 422 Unprocessable Entity Errors | Postmark Support Center
sources.claim: A 422 Unprocessable Entity error means Postmark successfully received your request, but the data inside that request is invalid or missing required information. When Postmark returns a 422 status code, the API response body contains a JSON object with a specific ErrorCode and a Message. Most 422 errors fall into categories like unverified sender signatures, missing required fields, invalid email formatting, or template model mismatches.

sources.title: Pydantic HTTP Exceptions: Handling Validation Errors in FastAPI - Orchestra
sources.claim: When a client sends data that doesn't conform to the Pydantic model's structure, FastAPI automatically returns a 422 Unprocessable Entity error detailing the validation errors.

sources.title: POST request to FastAPI endpoint produces error 422: "value is not a valid dict" | Sentry
sources.claim: A POST request to a FastAPI endpoint can fail with error code 422 and a detail message like {"loc":["body"],"msg":"value is not a valid dict","type":"type_error.dict"} if the submitted data is a list instead of a dictionary, not matching the expected Pydantic model.

sources.title: Postman POST request works but running a collection on the same request returns 422 error
sources.claim: The reason why a request with an attached file fails when run as a collection is probably the location of the attachment. Files uploaded through form-data must be placed in the working directory. Otherwise, the collection runner won't locate them and will send a request with no files attached. This results in 422. It's different from executing individual requests where files can be placed inside any directory.

sources.title: Error: Cache Service Responded with 422 - Postman Blog
sources.claim: HTTP 422 errors occur when your request is syntactically correct but contains invalid data. Unlike syntax errors that trigger 400 Bad Request, a 422 error means that the server understood your request structure but found problems with the actual content.

sources.title: 422 error. : r/FastAPI - Reddit
sources.claim: When FastAPI spits out a 422 error it also tries to give you advice to resolve the error in the content of the response. That should tell you what fields aren't valid. Example: {"detail":[{"loc":["body"],"msg":"value is not a valid dict","type":"type_error.dict"}]}. Double-check your multipart/form-data setup—make sure your Content-Type is set correctly with the proper boundary, and that you're naming your image field right.

sources.title: python - FastAPI server returns "422 unprocessable entity" - value_error.missing - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: A response having a 422 status code (i.e., Unprocessable entity) will have a response body that specifies the error message, telling exactly which part of your request is missing or doesn't match the expected format. If a FastAPI endpoint expects a JSON payload (Pydantic model) but receives data as a query parameter or incorrect JSON, it will return a 422 error.

sources.title: FastAPI POST 422 Errors: A Quick Fix Guide — The Pulse - The Wrasse Gazette
sources.claim: FastAPI, when it returns a 422 error, usually provides a detailed JSON response body explaining exactly what went wrong. This will typically list the fields that failed validation and the reason for the failure, e.g., "detail":[{"loc":["body","email"],"msg":"field required","type":"value_error.missing"}]. Pydantic is strict about data types.

sources.title: API Documentation: How to Write, Examples & Best Practices | Postman
sources.claim: API documentation includes detailed information about an API's available endpoints, methods, resources, authentication protocols, parameters, and headers, as well as examples of common requests and responses.

sources.title: Every POST Returned 422 — FastAPI Basics - YouTube
sources.claim: FastAPI returns 422 for validation errors. The JSON body looked perfect, but a Pydantic alias (item_name → name) meant the model expected the alias while the client sent the original name.

sources.title: POSTMAN Error 422 - Unprocessable Entity, with GitHub API. What is it and how do solve it?
sources.claim: Changing the Content-type from text/plain to application/json in the header fixed a 422 error for GitHub API interactions.

sources.title: Troubleshooting the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: If you omit required parameters or you use the wrong type for a parameter, you may receive a 422 Unprocessable Entity response and an "Invalid request" error message. The response body will include an errors property, which includes a code property to help you diagnose the problem. The formatting of a parameter might be invalid. Review the documentation for the specific endpoint to verify parameters.

sources.title: RequestSpecification (REST Assured 3.0.1 API) - javadoc.io
sources.claim: API documentation specifies request content types, headers, and parameters.

sources.title: Getting 422 Unprocessable Entity response from GitHub Search API · community · Discussion #139773
sources.claim: GitHub's Search API can return a 422 response with the message "Validation Failed" and errors like "The listed users and repositories cannot be searched either because the resources do not exist or you do not have permission to view them." when the query parameter 'q' has an invalid value.

sources.title: 422 Unprocessable Content Error - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: The Content-Type header and the request body format must match. It's recommended to use 'raw' with 'JSON' in Postman's Body tab for many APIs.

sources.title: 422 error when using a Pydantic model for query parameters with aliases #4842 - GitHub
sources.claim: FastAPI returns a 422 error with "loc":["query","field_name"],"msg":"field required","type":"value_error.missing" when a Pydantic model for query parameters with aliases is used and the expected field is missing.

sources.title: FastAPI file upload returns error 422 - Sentry
sources.claim: FastAPI file upload can return a 422 error with "loc":["body","data"],"msg":"field required","type":"value_error.missing" if the client-side file upload method doesn't match the FastAPI endpoint's expectation (e.g., expecting 'data' but receiving 'file').

sources.title: What Is an API Spec? - Akamai
sources.claim: An API spec outlines the various endpoints, methods, parameters, and data formats that developers can use. It serves as comprehensive documentation that outlines all the endpoints, methods, parameters, response structures, error handling processes, and authentication mechanisms.

sources.title: python - FastAPI server returns "422 unprocessable entity" - value_error.missing - Stack Overflow
sources.claim: The 422 unprocessable entity error tells exactly which part of your request doesn't match the expected format or is missing, with a 'loc' field often pointing to 'body' for missing body content.

sources.title: Provide the means to globally override the default 422 OpenAPI schema #3650 - GitHub
sources.claim: FastAPI exception handlers can return a JSONResponse with status code 422, often including 'detail' messages for validation errors.

sources.title: API documentation best practices guide Feb 2026 - Fern
sources.claim: API documentation must specify the supported authentication methods and explain where developers obtain credentials and how to pass them in requests. Request documentation should define data types, requirement status, and valid value ranges.

sources.title: search_issues returns 422 for repositories I own or administer, even though the repo exists and I have access · Issue #2206 · github/github-mcp-server
sources.claim: GitHub Search API returning 422 "Validation Failed" for query parameters (e.g., 'q') whose value may imply resources do not exist or user lacks permission, even if the repository exists and is accessible.

sources.title: FastAPI unit test returns 422 - Python Discussions
sources.claim: FastAPI's 422 Unprocessable Entity response body will say which input failed the validation done by Pydantic, e.g., if a string value cannot be converted into a datetime value.

sources.title: API | Standards Requests for Interpretation Guidelines - American Petroleum Institute
sources.claim: Official API documentation outlines specifications, standards, and required parameters for endpoints.

sources.title: Set working directory - Postman Docs
sources.claim: When you send a form-data or binary file with a request body, Postman saves a path to the test file as part of the collection. The file path is relative to your working directory. Storing files in your working directory ensures that requests in shared collections always work.

sources.title: When running collection via Postman Runner it fails, but when I send the requests sequentially in the same folder everything works as expected
sources.claim: When running a collection via Postman Runner, file uploads can fail if the file is not in the working directory, even if individual requests work.

sources.title: What is 422 Status Code “Unprocessable Entity” Error How To Fix It? - Sitechecker.pro
sources.claim: Invalid API requests with incorrect parameters, headers, or data in the request body can result in a 422 error.

sources.title: Manual Test Passing but failing in Collection Runner - #3 by allenheltondev - Help Hub
sources.claim: To fix file upload issues in Postman Collection Runner, the file being uploaded needs to be placed in the Postman working directory.

sources.title: Manual Test Passing but failing in Collection Runner - Help Hub - Postman Community
sources.claim: When executing a request with a file in Postman, if it passes manually but fails in the Collection Runner, it's often due to the file not being included in the collection runner's accessible directory (the working directory).

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.
Optional improvement: Verify if Postman itself explicitly states the 'loc' field in its console output for FastAPI errors, or if it's an interpretation of the FastAPI response shown in Postman.
Optional improvement: Add links to official documentation or primary sources for key claims.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
